$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Iraq infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Inflation and rates pressure in Iraq are probable (66%) over the next 30 days, stemming from 'Iraq infrastructure fragility state' feeding energy supply shock with 46% pressure and 68% state confirmation.
foil.perspective_strategic: The 66% probability of inflation and rates pressure, driven by 'Iraq infrastructure fragility state' and 46% energy supply shock, signals a significant economic vulnerability in Iraq.
foil.perspective_contrarian: Despite the 66% probability, the 75% confidence level and the need for more direct reporting on Iraq suggest that this outcome is not fully solidified and could be mitigated if infrastructure improvements or energy supply stabilization occurs.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Iraq becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 66%.
foil.top_signal: Iraq infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 46% pressure and 68% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
